title: Solving Equations of the type $|f(x)| = g(x)$ — Medium

questions.question: Solve the equation $|2x - 5| = |x + 4|$.
questions.explanation:
Case 1: $2x-5 = x+4 \implies x=9$. Case 2: $2x-5 = -(x+4) \implies 2x-5 = -x-4 \implies 3x=1 \implies x=1/3$.

**Common mistake:** Neglecting to distribute the negative sign across both terms in the bracket, e.g., using $-x+4$ instead of $-x-4$.

questions.question: Find the set of values of x for which $|3x + 2| = |2x - 8|$.
questions.explanation:
Case 1: $3x+2 = 2x-8 \implies x=-10$. Case 2: $3x+2 = -(2x-8) \implies 3x+2 = -2x+8 \implies 5x=6 \implies x=1.2$.

**Common mistake:** Arithmetic slips when handling the subtraction of terms across the equals sign.

questions.question: Solve $|5x - 1| = |x + 11|$.
questions.explanation:
Case 1: $5x-1 = x+11 \implies 4x=12 \implies x=3$. Case 2: $5x-1 = -x-11 \implies 6x=-10 \implies x=-5/3 \approx -1.67$.

**Common mistake:** Dividing 12 by 5 instead of 4, or 10 by 5 instead of 6.

questions.question: Solve the equation $|x - 7| = |4x + 8|$.
questions.explanation:
Case 1: $x-7 = 4x+8 \implies -3x=15 \implies x=-5$. Case 2: $x-7 = -4x-8 \implies 5x=-1 \implies x=-0.2$.

**Common mistake:** Sign errors while isolating x, particularly when dealing with $-3x = 15$.

questions.question: Find the solutions for $|2x + 10| = |3 - x|$.
questions.explanation:
Case 1: $2x+10 = 3-x \implies 3x=-7 \implies x=-7/3$. Case 2: $2x+10 = -(3-x) \implies 2x+10 = -3+x \implies x=-13$.

**Common mistake:** Incorrectly distributing the negative sign in Case 2, treating $-(3-x)$ as $-3-x$.
